---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Cmd+Shift+Enter*. 
Install Packages - James
```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#Libraries
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(skimr)
library(ggplot2)
library(corrplot)
library(naniar)   # for missing value visualizations
library(here)
```

Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Cmd+Option+I*.
EDA and Preprocessing - James
```{r}

#Preprocessing
#Load File

#James Directory:  df <- read_excel("/Users/jamesshoenhair/Documents/ADS503FinalProject_Mice_Protein_Expression/Data_Cortex_Nuclear.xls")
df <- read_excel("Data_Cortex_Nuclear.xls") #Veronica Directory

dim(df)          # 1080 rows, 82 columns
head(df)
str(df)

#initial Summary Statistics

summary(df)
skim(df) 

#Check for Null Values
colSums(is.na(df))

df %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "column", values_to = "missing") %>%
  filter(missing > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(missing))

#Handling Null Values Through Imputation

df_imputed <- df %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ifelse(is.na(.), median(., na.rm = TRUE), .)))

```

EDA and Pre-processing - James
```{r}
#Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
#Updated Summary Statistics
skim(df)

#Dat set Class Distributions
df %>%
  count(class) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = class, y = n, fill = class)) +
  geom_col() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Sample Count by Class")

#Genotype/Treatment/Behavior

table(df$Genotype)
table(df$Treatment)
table(df$Behavior)

#Protein Expression Distributions

df %>%
  select(DYRK1A_N, BDNF_N, APP_N, Tau_N) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything()) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value, fill = name)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~name, scales = "free") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Protein Expression Distributions")

#Correlation Heatmap
  
numeric_cols <- df %>% select(where(is.numeric))
corrplot(cor(numeric_cols, use = "complete.obs"),
         method = "color", tl.cex = 0.4, order = "hclust")

```
KNN Imputation - Veronica
```{r}
# Perform KNN imputation as new data set
install.packages("VIM"); library(VIM)
knn_imputed <- kNN(df, imp_var = FALSE)

# Compare Variable Correlations Between KNN and Median Imputation
numeric_med <- df_imputed[, sapply(df_imputed, is.numeric)]
cor_med <- cor(
  numeric_med,
  use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
)
cor_med <- as.data.frame(round(cor_med, 2))
knitr::kable(cor_med) # Median Imputation Correlation table
numeric_knn <- knn_imputed[, sapply(knn_imputed, is.numeric)]
cor_knn <- cor(
  numeric_knn,
  use = "pairwise.complete.obs"
)
cor_knn <- as.data.frame(round(cor_knn, 2))
knitr::kable(cor_knn) # KNN Imputation correlation table

# Slight differences in correlations: both methods can be inputted into models -> choose imputation method with best predictive outcome
```
Feature Selection - Veronica
```{r}
# Separate Predictors and Target for both datasets
y_knn <- knn_imputed$class
x_knn <- knn_imputed[, names(knn_imputed) != "class"]
y_med <- df_imputed$class
x_med <- df_imputed[, names(df_imputed) != "class"]

# Remove Mouse ID (not valuable)
x_knn <- x_knn[, names(x_knn) != "MouseID"]
x_med <- x_med[, names(x_med) != "MouseID"]

# One-Hot Encode Categorical Variables: Genotype, Treatment, and Behavior
dummies_knn <- model.matrix(~ Genotype + Treatment + Behavior - 1, data = x_knn)
dummies_med <- model.matrix(~ Genotype + Treatment + Behavior - 1, data = x_med)
x_knn <- cbind(x_knn[, !names(x_knn) %in% c("Genotype", "Treatment", "Behavior")], dummies_knn)
x_med <- cbind(x_med[, !names(x_med) %in% c("Genotype", "Treatment", "Behavior")], dummies_med)

# Validate One-Hot Encoding (Make sure we only have numerical variables)
str(x_knn)
str(x_med)

# Identify Near-Zero Variance Predictors
nzv_knn <- nearZeroVar(x_knn)
nzv_med <- nearZeroVar(x_med)
length(nzv_knn)
length(nzv_med) # There are no NZV predictors

# Identify and remove High Correlations 
cor_mat <- cor(x_med)
cor_mat_knn <- cor(x_knn)
high_med <- findCorrelation(cor_mat, cutoff = 0.90)
high_knn <- findCorrelation(cor_mat, cutoff = 0.90)
x_knn <- x_knn[, -high_knn]
x_med <- x_med[, -high_med]
length(x_knn)
length(x_med) # Reduced from 81 to 74 predictors
```

Cross-Validation/Test Train Split - Ramin
```{r}

```
Penalized Regression Models: Ridge, Lasso, & Elastic Net - Ramin
```{r}

```
Tree-Based Models: Random Forest & Gradient Boosting - Stephanie
```{r}

```
Logistic Regression & MARS - Veronica
```{r}

```
Evaluation Comparison Table - Veronica
```{r}

```
AUC-ROC Plot - Veronica
```{r}

```
When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Cmd+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file). 

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
